content.content:
- content: 'Payroll support investigations should be tied to the specific pay period

    and payment reference shown in the employee''s payroll records. Record the

    current platform status before giving guidance, and keep later payment

    events linked to the same original payment reference. General support

    notes do not determine whether a particular payment is still settling,

    has returned, or has entered a replacement lifecycle.

    '

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Track your pay and troubleshoot payment issues (for US employees)

  - url: https://support.gusto.com/article/100010018100000/track-your-pay-and-troubleshoot-payment-issues-for-us-employees

  - source: Gusto Help Center

  - domain: support.gusto.com


  ## Content


  # Track your pay and troubleshoot payment issues


  Employees can use Gusto to track upcoming payments, review payment details, and
  troubleshoot a paycheck that is missing or delayed.


  Your employer runs payroll and sets the pay schedule. Questions about when you are
  paid, how much you are paid, or why a paycheck amount differs from what you expected
  should normally start with your employer. Gusto can help with technical payment
  issues and with troubleshooting a missing direct deposit after payday.


  ## Troubleshoot a missing payment


  If the money is not in your account on payday, allow until 5 PM local time before
  treating the payment as missing. Banks may post direct deposits at different times
  during the business day.


  ### Check the correct Gusto account


  Make sure you are signed in to the correct Gusto profile. Employees with more than
  one Gusto account may need to switch accounts before reviewing the payment.


  ### Check the payment method


  Review your profile and the paystub for the relevant payroll.


  Having direct deposit configured in your profile does not guarantee that every payroll
  is paid by direct deposit. An employer can choose a different payment method for
  a particular payroll.


  The paystub shows whether that payroll was paid by direct deposit or check. A payment
  marked as a check will not appear in a bank account; the employer is responsible
  for delivering the paper check.


  ### Review the payment while it is still being tracked


  The pay tracker shows the real-time status of upcoming payments. It may show:


  - when the employer submitted payroll;

  - the estimated payday;

  - the company''s direct-deposit speed;

  - the final payment method after payroll is processed;

  - the bank accounts selected for the processed payment;

  - whether the employee needs to update bank information or contact the employer.


  The estimated payday may change if the employer submits payroll earlier or later
  than planned.


  On payday, the bank may post the money at any point during the business day, up
  to 5 PM local time. The timing depends on the bank.


  To view the pay tracker in a web browser:


  1. Sign in to Gusto.

  2. On the Home page, find Upcoming paychecks.

  3. Select Track paycheck status.


  In the mobile app, look for the next scheduled payday on the Home page and select
  Track paycheck status.


  ## How direct deposit moves through the payment process


  A direct-deposit payment involves several stages:


  1. The employer submits payroll to Gusto.

  2. Gusto sends the direct-deposit payment electronically to the employee''s bank.

  3. Gusto may send a payday email confirming that payroll was submitted.

  4. The paystub appears in the employee''s Gusto account.

  5. The bank posts the funds to the account, which may happen later in the business
  day.


  A payday email confirming payroll submission does not mean that the funds have already
  arrived in the bank account. Likewise, seeing a paystub means there is a payroll
  record, but the employee should still use the payment status and bank timing information
  to determine what has happened to the actual deposit.


  ## If the payment has not arrived


  If no bank error has been reported, banks can take up to three business days after
  the check date to report an error. Gusto may need to wait for that bank response
  before it can help resolve the issue.


  After that period, an employee can contact Gusto through the Help section of the
  account. If no bank error has been reported, Gusto may provide an ACH Trace ID.
  The employee can give that tracking number to the bank to help locate the payment.


  Employees can also sign in to the current bank account or call the bank to ask whether
  a payment is pending.


  If the employee recently changed banks, the payment may have been sent using the
  account information associated with the payroll when it was processed. Check the
  previous account first.


  If a later bank response confirms that the payment was returned, use the separate
  Gusto guidance "Edit bank and personal information (for US employees and contractors)"
  to determine the returned-payment timeline and the conditions for any resend.


  ## Still need help


  Contact the employer first to confirm:


  - the expected pay date;

  - the payment method for the relevant payroll;

  - the bank account details associated with the payment;

  - the payment details shown for that payroll.


  If the employer cannot resolve the issue, the employer can contact Gusto support
  for more detailed information about the payment or any reported errors.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Edit bank and personal information (for US employees and contractors)

  - url: https://support.gusto.com/article/137880230100000/edit-bank-and-personal-information-for-us-employees-and-contractors

  - source: Gusto Help Center

  - domain: support.gusto.com


  ## Content


  # Edit bank and personal information


  US employees and contractors can manage certain bank-account details in Gusto. The
  timing of a bank change matters because a payroll may already have been submitted
  using the payment information associated with that payroll.


  ## Changes made after payroll is submitted


  If bank information is changed after payroll has been submitted but before payday,
  the funds may still go to the old account. A bank change does not rewrite the destination
  already attached to a payment that is in progress.


  For a payment problem, first distinguish between:


  - a payment that is still being processed or is waiting for a bank response;

  - a payment for which Gusto has received a bank error;

  - a payment that has gone back to the company''s bank account after the error was
  not resolved.


  These states have different consequences.


  ## If Gusto receives a bank error


  When Gusto receives a bank error while trying to deposit pay, Gusto emails the employee
  and sends reminders every three business days.


  If the issue is not fixed within 12 business days, the pay goes back to the company''s
  bank account. At that point the original deposit is no longer simply an unresolved
  payment still moving toward the employee''s account.


  Gusto automatically tries to resend the payment when the employee:


  - updates the existing bank information; or

  - fully removes the old bank account and adds a new one.


  The resend applies to a payment that has entered the bank-error process. It is different
  from continuing to wait for an original deposit that has not yet received a final
  bank outcome.


  ## If no bank error has been reported yet


  A bank can take up to three business days after the check date to report an error.
  Gusto may need to wait for that response before it can help fix the issue.


  After three business days, an employee who still needs help can contact Gusto from
  the Help section of the account. If no error has been reported, Gusto may provide
  an ACH Trace ID that the employee can give to the bank to help track and reconcile
  the payment.


  This means that the absence of funds is not, by itself, proof that the payment has
  already failed or been returned. The current payment state matters.


  ## Removing a bank account


  Removing a bank account is a separate configuration action and must be done in a
  web browser.


  If an employee removes the only bank account on file, payrolls submitted while no
  bank account is available are classified as paper-check payments instead of direct
  deposit.


  If an employee cannot access the old bank account or cannot complete the verification
  step needed to remove it, the employee should contact the employer''s Gusto admin.
  The admin may need to contact Gusto support.


  ## Missing-payment checks


  For a missing payment, confirm the relevant payment method and payment state before
  changing bank information.


  If a direct-deposit payment is still waiting for a bank response, the process is
  not the same as a payment with a reported bank error. If the payment has moved through
  the bank-error process and gone back to the company account, it should no longer
  be treated as an original deposit that is simply still pending.

  '

args.reason: Determine whether the returned paycheck has been reissued or remains unresolved after the confirmed bank return.

content.content:
I checked the payment history for the pay period **June 16–30, 2026**:

- Expected deposit date: **July 3, 2026**
- Original payment ID: **pay_6QF2M9**
- The payment was transmitted to the direct-deposit destination on file, and that destination matched your account configuration at payroll time.
- The bank initially reported no error, but later reported a **confirmed return on July 7**. The funds were returned to the company’s account.
- A replacement record exists, but its status is **active—resend not started**.

So this is no longer simply a delayed deposit. The original payment failed and was returned, and the replacement payment still needs to be initiated.

### What to do now

1. **Check with your bank** to determine why the deposit was rejected—especially if the account was closed, changed, or the routing/account information was incorrect.
2. **Verify your direct-deposit details** in the payroll system. Don’t change them just to trigger a resend if they are already correct.
3. **Contact your employer/payroll administrator today** and give them:
   - Pay period: **June 16–30, 2026**
   - Original payment ID: **pay_6QF2M9**
   - Return event: **July 7, 2026**
   - Reissue ID: **reissue_4K7**
   
   Ask them to correct or confirm the bank information and start the replacement payment.
4. If the bank information is wrong, update the existing account—or remove the old account and add the correct one—according to your payroll provider’s process. The provider’s guidance says this can trigger an automatic resend for a payment already in the bank-error process, but your employer/payroll team should confirm the correct action first.

The payroll provider’s guidance also recommends contacting the employer first for the payment method, destination details, and resolution of returned payments. [Missing-payment guidance](https://support.gusto.com/article/100010018100000/track-your-pay-and-troubleshoot-payment-issues-for-us-employees) · [Returned-payment guidance](https://support.gusto.com/article/137880230100000/edit-bank-and-personal-information-for-us-employees-and-contractors)

final_answer:
I checked the payment history for the pay period **June 16–30, 2026**:

- Expected deposit date: **July 3, 2026**
- Original payment ID: **pay_6QF2M9**
- The payment was transmitted to the direct-deposit destination on file, and that destination matched your account configuration at payroll time.
- The bank initially reported no error, but later reported a **confirmed return on July 7**. The funds were returned to the company’s account.
- A replacement record exists, but its status is **active—resend not started**.

So this is no longer simply a delayed deposit. The original payment failed and was returned, and the replacement payment still needs to be initiated.

### What to do now

1. **Check with your bank** to determine why the deposit was rejected—especially if the account was closed, changed, or the routing/account information was incorrect.
2. **Verify your direct-deposit details** in the payroll system. Don’t change them just to trigger a resend if they are already correct.
3. **Contact your employer/payroll administrator today** and give them:
   - Pay period: **June 16–30, 2026**
   - Original payment ID: **pay_6QF2M9**
   - Return event: **July 7, 2026**
   - Reissue ID: **reissue_4K7**
   
   Ask them to correct or confirm the bank information and start the replacement payment.
4. If the bank information is wrong, update the existing account—or remove the old account and add the correct one—according to your payroll provider’s process. The provider’s guidance says this can trigger an automatic resend for a payment already in the bank-error process, but your employer/payroll team should confirm the correct action first.

The payroll provider’s guidance also recommends contacting the employer first for the payment method, destination details, and resolution of returned payments. [Missing-payment guidance](https://support.gusto.com/article/100010018100000/track-your-pay-and-troubleshoot-payment-issues-for-us-employees) · [Returned-payment guidance](https://support.gusto.com/article/137880230100000/edit-bank-and-personal-information-for-us-employees-and-contractors)
